title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create virtual environment and install all required packages for LASER-SCL experiments

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.0+ with CUDA: pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118
steps.step3: Install scientific packages: pip install numpy scipy scikit-learn matplotlib seaborn pandas
steps.step4: Install utility packages: pip install tqdm tensorboard pillow
steps.step5: Verify CUDA availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step6: Create project structure: mkdir -p src data results/figures results/checkpoints results/logs

title: Download and Prepare CIFAR Datasets
description: Download CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and CIFAR-100-N datasets; create train/val splits; save noise indices for reproducibility

steps.step1: Download CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 using torchvision.datasets: python -c "import torchvision; torchvision.datasets.CIFAR10(root='./data', download=True); torchvision.datasets.CIFAR100(root='./data', download=True)"
steps.step2: Download CIFAR-100-N (human noise) from https://github.com/UCSC-REAL/cifar-10-100n and extract to data/cifar-100-noisy/
steps.step3: Create train/val split (90/10) for CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 using fixed seed 42; save split indices to data/splits/
steps.step4: Generate synthetic noise with fixed seeds [42, 123, 456] for reproducibility:
steps.step4a: Symmetric noise: Randomly flip labels to other classes uniformly. Generate noise at 20%, 40%, 60% rates for CIFAR-10
steps.step4b: Asymmetric noise: Flip labels to similar classes (e.g., truck->automobile, bird->airplane). Generate at 20%, 40% for CIFAR-10
steps.step4c: CIFAR-100 noise: Generate symmetric noise at 20%, 40% only (focus on key noise level 40% to save compute)
steps.step5: Save all noise indices as numpy arrays: data/noise_indices/cifar10_sym_40_seed42.npy, etc.
steps.step6: Compute and save dataset statistics (mean, std) from training set only for normalization
steps.step7: Verify data integrity: Load samples and check label distribution after noise injection

title: Implement and Run Vanilla SupCon Baseline
description: Implement standard Supervised Contrastive Learning as the primary baseline

steps.step1: Implement ResNet-18 encoder in src/models.py with CIFAR-compatible stem (3x3 conv, stride 1 instead of 7x7 stride 2)
steps.step2: Implement standard SupCon loss in src/losses.py: L_i = -log[exp(z_i · z_p/τ) / sum_{a in A(i)} exp(z_i · z_a/τ)]
steps.step3: Implement training loop in src/train.py with SGD optimizer, lr=0.5, momentum=0.9, weight_decay=1e-4, cosine annealing
steps.step4: Implement linear evaluation protocol in src/eval.py: Freeze encoder, train linear classifier for 100 epochs
steps.step5: Run SupCon on CIFAR-10 clean: 500 epochs, batch_size=256, temperature=0.1, projection_dim=128, seeds=[42, 123, 456]
steps.step6: Run SupCon on CIFAR-10 with 40% symmetric noise: 3 seeds, save final accuracy and per-epoch loss history
steps.step7: Run SupCon on CIFAR-100 with 40% symmetric noise: 3 seeds (prioritize this for main comparison)

title: Implement and Run Simple Reweighting Baselines
description: Implement loss-based reweighting baselines to validate unique value of ELP

steps.step1: Implement SupCon + Loss Reweighting (LR): weight_i = 1 / (loss_i + epsilon), normalize weights per batch
steps.step2: Implement SupCon + Inverse Loss (IL): weight_i = loss_i / mean(loss), promotes hard samples
steps.step3: Implement SupCon + Focal Reweighting (FR): weight_i = (1 - p_i)^gamma where p_i = exp(-loss_i), gamma=2.0
steps.step4: Run SupCon+LR on CIFAR-10 40% noise: 3 seeds, this is the CRITICAL baseline for success criterion
steps.step5: Run SupCon+IL on CIFAR-10 40% noise: 1-2 seeds (less critical, for completeness)
steps.step6: Run SupCon+LR on CIFAR-100 40% noise: 2 seeds, compare against LASER-SCL
steps.step7: Save detailed loss and weight statistics per epoch for analysis

title: Implement and Run UNICON Baseline
description: Implement UNICON (two-network co-teaching with uniform selection) for comparison

steps.step1: Implement two-network co-teaching framework in src/unicon.py
steps.step2: Implement Jensen-Shannon divergence-based selection for identifying clean samples
steps.step3: Implement contrastive learning on selected clean samples
steps.step4: Run UNICON on CIFAR-10 40% symmetric noise: 2 seeds (reduced from 3 due to compute cost)
steps.step5: Run UNICON on CIFAR-100 40% symmetric noise: 2 seeds
steps.step6: Track selection precision/recall to compare with LASER-SCL's continuous weighting

title: Synthetic ELP Validation Experiment
description: Validate that ELP distinguishes clean from noisy samples before full method implementation. THIS IS THE FIRST EXPERIMENT TO RUN for preliminary evidence.

steps.step1: Train vanilla SupCon on CIFAR-10 with 40% symmetric noise for 100 epochs (shorter run)
steps.step2: Track per-sample loss history with window W=10 for all training samples
steps.step3: Compute ELP for each sample: ELP = -Cov(loss_history, step_indices) / Var(step_indices)
steps.step4: Compute AUC-ROC for: (a) ELP-based clean vs noisy discrimination, (b) Loss-based discrimination
steps.step5: Generate trajectory visualization plots: Loss vs Epoch for clean samples, noisy samples
steps.step6: Statistical test: t-test comparing ELP distributions of clean vs noisy samples
steps.step7: Report: ELP AUC-ROC, Loss AUC-ROC, and whether ELP significantly outperforms (should be >0.05 AUC difference)

title: Implement and Run LASER-SCL (Full Method)
description: Implement complete LASER-SCL with ELP, predicted future loss, and curriculum scheduling

steps.step1: Implement ELP computation module in src/laser_scl.py: Track loss history per sample in sliding window W=10
steps.step2: Implement predicted future loss: l_hat = mean(loss_history) - ELP * Delta, where Delta=5
steps.step3: Implement curriculum schedule: mu(t) = mu_min + (mu_max - mu_min) * (t/T)^rho, with mu_min=0.3, mu_max=0.7, rho=2
steps.step4: Implement sample weighting: w_i = 1 / (1 + exp(k * (l_hat_i - mu(t)))), k=10
steps.step5: Integrate weighted SupCon loss: L_total = sum_i w_i * L_i^SupCon
steps.step6: Run LASER-SCL on CIFAR-10 clean (sanity check): 1 seed, should match vanilla SupCon
steps.step7: Run LASER-SCL on CIFAR-10 40% symmetric noise: 3 seeds [42, 123, 456] - CRITICAL for success criterion
steps.step8: Run LASER-SCL on CIFAR-10 20% and 60% symmetric noise: 2 seeds each (if time permits)
steps.step9: Run LASER-SCL on CIFAR-100 40% symmetric noise: 3 seeds - PRIMARY BENCHMARK
steps.step10: Run LASER-SCL on CIFAR-100-N (real noise): 2 seeds
steps.step11: Save all loss trajectories, ELP values, and weight distributions per epoch for analysis

title: Ablation Study: Component Analysis
description: Systematically ablate each novel component to validate its contribution

steps.step1: Ablation 1 - No Curriculum (Fixed Threshold): mu(t) = 0.5 constant, keep ELP prediction. Run on CIFAR-100 40% noise, 2 seeds
steps.step2: Ablation 2 - No ELP (Current Loss Only): Use l_bar instead of l_hat, keep curriculum. Run on CIFAR-100 40% noise, 2 seeds
steps.step3: Ablation 3 - Neither (Static Weighting): Fixed threshold + current loss = simple loss-based weighting. Run on CIFAR-100 40% noise, 2 seeds
steps.step4: Ablation 4 - ELP Only (No Curriculum, No Prediction): Use raw ELP scores as weights without curriculum. Run on CIFAR-100 40%, 2 seeds
steps.step5: For each ablation, compute accuracy and compare against full LASER-SCL
steps.step6: Report performance drop for each component; each should contribute >=0.5% to pass success criterion

title: Hyperparameter Sensitivity Analysis
description: Test robustness to key hyperparameters window size W and prediction horizon Delta

steps.step1: Vary window size W: Test W in [5, 10, 20] on CIFAR-10 40% noise, 1 seed each
steps.step2: Vary prediction horizon Delta: Test Delta in [2, 5, 10] on CIFAR-10 40% noise, 1 seed each
steps.step3: Vary curriculum sharpness rho: Test rho in [1, 2, 3] on CIFAR-10 40% noise, 1 seed each
steps.step4: Report accuracy sensitivity; method should be relatively robust (variation <1% across reasonable values)

title: Statistical Significance Testing and Results Compilation
description: Perform rigorous statistical tests and compile all results into summary tables

steps.step1: Load all experimental results from JSON files
steps.step2: Compute mean and standard deviation for each method-dataset-noise combination across seeds
steps.step3: Perform paired t-test between LASER-SCL and each baseline on CIFAR-100 40% noise (main comparison)
steps.step4: Verify success criterion 1: LASER-SCL mean accuracy - SupCon+LR mean accuracy >= 2% on CIFAR-100 40% noise
steps.step5: Verify success criterion 2: Each ablation drop >= 0.5% compared to full method
steps.step6: Verify success criterion 3: ELP AUC-ROC > Loss AUC-ROC for clean/noisy discrimination
steps.step7: Verify success criterion 4: Compute training time overhead (<20% to pass)
steps.step8: Create summary table: Method vs Accuracy on CIFAR-10/100 at various noise rates
steps.step9: Create comparison with literature: Report MW-Net, UNICON, DivideMix numbers from papers for reference
steps.step10: Report all results with mean ± std format

title: Generate Paper Figures
description: Create publication-quality figures for the paper

steps.step1: Figure 1 - Method Overview: Diagram showing ELP computation, predicted future loss, curriculum scheduling
steps.step2: Figure 2 - Main Results Bar Chart: Compare LASER-SCL vs baselines on CIFAR-10/100 at different noise rates, include error bars
steps.step3: Figure 3 - Loss Trajectories: Plot loss curves for 10 random clean samples and 10 random noisy samples during training, show ELP difference
steps.step4: Figure 4 - Ablation Study: Bar chart showing accuracy for full method vs each ablation
steps.step5: Figure 5 - Weight Distribution Evolution: Histogram of sample weights at epochs 50, 150, 300, 450 showing curriculum progression
steps.step6: Figure 6 - ELP vs Accuracy Correlation: Scatter plot showing ELP values vs final prediction correctness
steps.step7: Save all figures as high-resolution PNG (300 DPI) and PDF for paper submission

title: Final Report and Refutation Check
description: Generate final report documenting all findings and whether hypotheses were confirmed or refuted

steps.step1: Compile all experimental results into structured report
steps.step2: Evaluate against confirmation criteria: Did LASER-SCL outperform SupCon+LR by >=2%? Did each component contribute >=0.5%? Did ELP achieve higher AUC-ROC?
steps.step3: Evaluate against refutation criteria: If LASER-SCL performed worse than SupCon+LR, or ELP provides no benefit over current loss, document this honestly
steps.step4: Generate conclusions: Summarize key findings, limitations, and future work
steps.step5: Save hyperparameters used to results/hyperparameters.json for reproducibility
steps.step6: Create execution log documenting any deviations from plan
